code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Strong dissatisfaction or anger
    - Product is defective, unsafe, or immediately returned
    - Safety hazards automatically warrant 1 star
    - Explicit negative declarations: "terrible," "junk," "don't buy"

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - More negative than positive
    - Core functionality compromised by major flaws
    - Regret language: "waste of money," "false advertising"
    - Pattern: "Works BUT [serious issue]..."

    3 Stars (Neutral / Mixed):
    - Balanced pros and cons
    - Conditional utility: "good for occasional use"
    - Temporary solution: "better than nothing"
    - Non-essential flaws with basic functionality intact

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Clear satisfaction with specific criticisms
    - Explicit star deductions for usability issues
    - Maintained recommendation despite flaws
    - Example: "Almost perfect except for..."

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Enthusiastic endorsement with minor/non-impactful critiques
    - Explicit "5 stars overall" declarations override aspect ratings
    - Multi-feature excellence with one average aspect
    - Transformative benefits: "life-changing," "exceeded expectations"

    EDGE CASE HANDLING:
    - "Still 5 stars despite..." → 5 (minor drawbacks with strong endorsement)
    - Multi-aspect reviews → 5 if majority features excel
    - Star deduction + recommendation → 5 if conclusion is enthusiastic
    - Temporary usefulness → 3 regardless of positive adjectives
    - Safety issues override all positives → 1
    - Price praise with "worth every penny" phrasing → 5
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: This mattress gets 4 stars for comfort but 5 overall - amazing features make it exceptional!
<Reasoning>Explicit multi-aspect evaluation with overriding positive conclusion.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: UPDATE: Worked great initially but broke after 2 months. Not worth the price.
<Reasoning>Negative update overrides initial positive experience. Short lifespan indicates poor quality.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: Solves my scraping problem perfectly! Deducting one star because handle isn't dishwasher safe.
<Reasoning>Functional excellence with specific convenience drawback warrants 4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: These towels look luxurious but require 3 washes to become absorbent. Expected better at this price.
<Reasoning>Aesthetic appeal countered by functional deficiency. Mixed with value concerns.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "Gets 4 stars for comfort but there's so many other features that it deserves 5 overall. Highly recommend!"
<Reasoning>Explicit overall rating declaration overrides specific aspect score.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: The perfect organizer! Deducting one star for assembly instructions but still love it.
<Reasoning>Minor issue doesn't diminish strong endorsement and satisfaction.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: Makes perfect smoothies but overheats constantly. Not reliable for daily use.
<Reasoning>Core functionality compromised by reliability issues despite good results.</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: Ideal for guest rooms but too flimsy for main bed. Does the job temporarily.
<Reasoning>Conditional utility with explicit temporary use case.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: Catastrophic failure after 1 week. Dangerous sparks - immediate return!
<Reasoning>Safety hazard overrides any potential positives.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (36 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 14
  gold 5 -> 4: 13
  gold 4 -> 3: 5
  gold 1 -> 2: 2
  gold 3 -> 2: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "The color pattern here is very pleasant. The rug looks great. I wish I had tiled my whole floor like this. The non-stick feature is sprayed on rather that woven into the rug. I hope that it holds up."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "We are both tough on dish towels and use them constantly. Not only do we cook 90% of our meals at home but we also use fresh fruits and veggies with pretty much every meal and that means a lot of washing, drying and carrying things around...why carry..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I like this towel. It is 100% Turkish cotton and measures 27 inches by 55 inches. The dobby weave is somewhat intricate so you are going to get some good absorbency. This towel also comes in a bath sheet, which is competively priced with the local st..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "So far this blender is working well for me. I've made a few strawberry banana smoothies for my kids and it's worked fine each time. One thing that wasn't skimped on was the looks. The build design of these blenders is very sharp looking, having a bri..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "I always hate not being able to get the last remains out of a jar or a can. This definitely takes care of that problem and with ease. Pour out what you can and simply use this to scrape the rest out. The reason I'm deducting a star is because it isn'..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "EDIT: A year of use later it's still humming along and doing its job just fine. Sometimes it seems like it doesn't pick up quickly that it should be upping its speed on its own in the auto setting, but maybe it's just taking longer for the air to rea..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "I bought one of those Pyrex sets a few years ago.  You know, the one that came with the blue lids and some of the containers were rectangular --large and small-- while others were round (like these).  Well, turns out that the ones I used the most wer..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "Give Thanks Plaid is such a pretty fall pattern. The colors look great together and it can even be used going into the Christmas season if you're pairing it with rustic, country holiday decor. This tablecloth looks AMAZING with red or green plates, b..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "I would consider this 6 piece set to be hand towels. They are 16 inches by 30 inches. They are not the thinnest towels I have ever seen, and they are far from the plushest. I checked the description again and they have bath towels and wash cloths. Th..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "If there's anything you ever thought you wanted or needed in a trash can, this one has it. First, it's a semi-round, so it seems to have a smaller footprint than traditional round cans. Second, it has a silent opening and soft closing, which is great..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 36 errors, 12 borderline out of 80 examples."}
